Spanning 341,250 square feet and featuring 35 dock doors and 2 drive-in bays, this facility, established in 1994, serves as a critical logistics hub for various sectors, including automotive, defense, and food raw materials.

Founded in 1958, the company began as a one-man, one-truck operation in Oxford, Alabama, and has since evolved into a comprehensive logistics and trucking provider, demonstrating significant growth from its humble beginnings.

value.high: BR Williams is the trading name associated with the company.

value.all: B.R. Williams

description.all: B.R. Williams, originally founded in 1958, underwent significant restructuring in 2024 when its operations were consolidated under the brand BRW after being acquired by Haney and White Enterprise. This strategic move indicates the company's commitment to growth and expanding its logistics capabilities, now operating with over 300 employees and a fleet of 175 tractors.

description.high: BR Williams operates within two primary NAICS classifications: 484121 for General Freight Trucking, Long-Distance, Truckload, indicating a focus on large freight transport, and 488510 for Freight Transportation Arrangement, reflecting their role as a logistics broker. This structure suggests a strong emphasis on asset-backed transport and freight brokerage services.

value.high: The ownership structure is now under Haney and White Enterprise, which acquired BR Williams in 2024.

description.high: The ownership of BR Williams transitioned to Haney and White Enterprise, which acquired the company along with its subsidiaries, BR Williams Trucking and BR Williams LTL Logistics, in 2024. This acquisition led to the formation of a consolidated entity named BRW, merging four major transportation companies to enhance logistics operations and expand service offerings. The integration is projected to generate immediate annual revenues nearing $100 million, with a goal of reaching $250 million by 2030, indicating a significant growth trajectory for the new entity.
